surface.name: Chronosphere developer metrics query with Query Builder
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): select the first developer path when one exists rather than an administrator-only route. The general guide lists Query telemetry data first for developers and recommends Query Builder; a returned graph or table is the fastest documented developer terminal.

alternatives_considered.name: OpenTelemetry Collector
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Docs recommend Chronosphere Collector for Prometheus discovery/scrape workloads.

alternatives_considered.name: Kubernetes DaemonSet Collector
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Recommended inside Kubernetes, but requires a cluster; standalone is faster when Kubernetes is not already available.

alternatives_considered.name: Administrator Collector ingestion
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This requires SysAdmin access, service-account credentials, Collector installation, and telemetry configuration; the policy explicitly prefers the first developer path.

entry_point.developer_intent: Authenticate to an existing Observability Platform tenant and run a first Prometheus metric query with Query Builder.
assumed_prior_state: An Observability Platform tenant exists or can be obtained through Chronosphere's commercial onboarding.
Prometheus metrics are already ingested and visible to the developer.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Query telemetry data with Query Builder.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer selects an available Prometheus metric and runs a query in Metrics Explorer.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Results section displays matching series and values in Table and Graph.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Click Run to run the query; the graph updates.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The policy resolves the role choice by taking the first developer path. A returned query result is the earliest demonstrated developer value; saving, dashboarding, alerting, and deeper investigation follow later.

prerequisites.requirement: Access to an organization-specific Chronosphere Observability Platform tenant.
Developer permission to use Metrics Explorer and query at least one metric.
Prometheus metrics are already ingested into the tenant.

primary_path.action: Open Get started with Observability Platform and select the first developer task, Query telemetry data.
primary_path.output: The Metrics Explorer and Query Builder route is selected.
primary_path.success_signal: Query Builder is recommended for constructing PromQL.

primary_path.action: Obtain organization-provisioned access and authenticate at the tenant URL.
details: Account enrollment is one opaque access step under policy; no unlisted signup fields are asserted.
primary_path.input: The organization tenant URL and provisioned identity
primary_path.output: The Observability Platform home page opens.
primary_path.success_signal: The authenticated home page is displayed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An administrator must provision the user or enable the SSO identity.

primary_path.action: Open Metrics Explorer and click Builder for a query.
details: The route assumes Prometheus metrics are already ingested.
primary_path.input: An authenticated tenant with metric data
primary_path.success_signal: Metric, label, function, and rollup controls are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Ask an administrator to ingest or grant access to metrics if none are available.

primary_path.action: Select an available metric and any desired labels or rollup.
details: The builder suggests metric names, labels, values, and functions.
primary_path.output: A valid PromQL expression is constructed.
primary_path.success_signal: The expression appears in the query field.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If type information is unavailable, select the rollup manually.

primary_path.success_signal: The graph updates and Results shows matching series and values in Graph and Table.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Adjust the metric, labels, time range, or expression and run again.

friction_gates.description: A provisioned tenant is required; public docs start after authenticate rather than self-serve signup fields.
Creating the service-account API token requires SysAdmin team membership.

sources.title: Standalone Collector installation to retrieve metrics
Create PromQL queries with the query builder

uncertainties.question: How is a new Observability Platform tenant provisioned for a developer with no existing Chronosphere access?
uncertainties.impact: The path assumes an existing tenant and authenticated session before Collector install.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Public get-started docs begin at authenticate and do not publish a self-serve tenant signup sequence.
